system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

vertices

    (25 -75 25)   // 14
    (25 -75 -25)  // 15

    (-500 -75 25) // 16
    (-500 -75 -25) // 17
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)        (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)   // inlet duct
    hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11)      (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)   // floor bend base
    hex (4 7 11 5 12 15 10 8)    (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)   // ceiling bend entry
    hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)  (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) // main bend
    hex (12 15 16 10 14 17 18 13)(100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) // outlet transition
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 17 18 13)
        );
    }

    upperWall

        faces
        (
            (0 4 5 1)
            (4 12 13 5)
            (12 14 15 13)
            (14 16 17 15)
        );
    }

            (11 10 8 7)
            (10 14 15 11)
            (15 18 19 10)
        );
    }

            (13 12 8 10)

            (11 4 8 10)
            (10 15 14 11)

);

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(4 7 6 5) 

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_20260516_195157_run2/runs/squareBend/3/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 42.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block has inward-pointing faces

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11) ... has inward-pointing faces
    4(4 7 6 5) 4(4 9 13 8) 4(8 9 10 11) 4(4 13 12 7)
```

**Diagnosis:**

The block `hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11)` is invalid because the vertex ordering produces inward-pointing faces. The problem is the geometric relationship between vertices 4-7 (at x=0) and vertices 8-11 (at x=25). 

Let me trace the coordinates:
- Vertices 4=(0,25,25), 5=(0,25,-25), 6=(0,-25,-25), 7=(0,-25,25) — these form a rectangle in the x=0 plane going CCW when viewed from +x, meaning the normal points in **-x**.
- Vertices 8=(25,-25,25), 9=(25,-25,-25), 10=(25,0,-25), 11=(25,0,25) — these form a quadrilateral in the x=25 plane. The winding (8→9→10→11) gives a normal that points in **+x**.

Since the two faces have opposing normals (+x vs -x), the block is inverted. To fix this, swap the vertex ordering so that both faces have normals pointing in the same direction (from the x=0 face toward the x=25 face).

**Resolution:**

Change Block 1 from:
```
hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11)
```
to:
```
hex (4 7 6 5 8 11 10 9)
```

This reverses the winding so that:
- Face `(4 7 6 5)` at x=0 now has its normal pointing toward x=25
- Face `(8 11 10 9)` at x=25 has its normal pointing toward x=0

The boundary face definitions also need updating since vertex pairs have swapped. Specifically, any face referencing vertices 4↔5 or 8↔9 or 6↔7 or 10↔11 needs correction. For example, the `upperWall` face `(0 4 5 1)` becomes `(0 5 4 1)`, and the `lowerWall` face `(3 7 6 2)` becomes `(3 6 7 2)`.

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points"

**Diagnosis:**

This is a cascading error from `blockMesh` failing. It will resolve automatically once `blockMesh` succeeds.

## Summary

The **only fix** is in **`system/blockMeshDict`**: change Block 1 from `hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11)` to `hex (4 7 6 5 8 11 10 9)`. Additionally, update the corresponding boundary face definitions to match the new vertex ordering (swap vertex pairs 4↔5, 6↔7, 8↔9, 10↔11 where needed). All other files remain correct and ready for execution once the mesh is successfully generated.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Change Block 1 vertex ordering from hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11) to hex (4 7 6 5 8 11 10 9) to fix inward-pointing faces; Update upperWall face (0 4 5 1) to (0 5 4 1); Update lowerWall face (3 7 6 2) to (3 6 7 2); Update leftWall face (1 5 6 2) to (1 6 5 2); Update rightWall face (0 3 7 4) to (0 4 7 3); Update outlet face (16 17 18 13) remains unchanged')]
